executiveSummary.text: Delta Air Lines offers an attractive combination of strong pricing power, disciplined capacity management, and an improving balance sheet, trading at a significant discount to intrinsic value on a P/E of under 10x, and is rated HOLD.

businessModel.text:
Delta is the largest US airline by market capitalisation and a leading global carrier. Its revenue is derived from scheduled passenger service, cargo transportation, and ancillary services including loyalty programme monetisation. The business model has structurally shifted post-pandemic toward capacity discipline and premium revenue mix:
    
      Mainline Passenger Revenue~85% of total revenue; basic economy to premium delta one
      Cargo~3% of revenue; freight and mail
      Loyalty Programme (SkyMiles)~8% of revenue; co-brand credit card and retail partnerships
      Maintenance, Repair & Overhaul (MRO)~4% of revenue; delta techops serves third-party airlines
    
    Delta's competitive moat lies in its premium route network, superior operational reliability, and the SkyMiles loyalty programme, which is one of the most valuable co-brand relationships in US consumer finance. The MRO business (Delta TechOps) is a high-margin, underappreciated earnings contributor.

thesisEvaluation.text:
ScenarioScoreProbabilityCommentary
    
      Bull8630%Premium demand sustains; corporate travel recovery adds incremental yield; fuel costs remain benign; EPS reaches $8.50-9.50; shares re-rate to 12-14x, reaching $95-110.
    
    
      Base7850%Steady demand environment; capacity discipline maintained; MRO growth offsets modest unit revenue pressure; EPS grows to $7.50-8.50; shares reach $80-88 on 10-11x.
    
    
      Bear6020%Recession reduces leisure and corporate travel simultaneously; low-cost carrier competition intensifies; fuel price spike; EPS falls to $4-5; shares test $38-45.
    
  
  
    Conviction Score: 86x0.30 + 78x0.50 + 60x0.20 = 78.6
    The bull/base scenarios score strongly; the P/E of under 10x provides a significant margin of safety even in the base case.

base.scenario: base scenarios score strongly; the P/E of under 10x provides a significant margin of safety even in the base case.

keyRisks.text: # Risk Description 1 Fuel price volatility Jet fuel is Delta's second-largest cost item; a 20% spike in fuel prices without corresponding ticket price increases would materially compress margins given

whoShouldOwn.text: Ideal For Avoid Owners Value and yield investors attracted to the under-10x P/E and improving free cash flow generation Cyclical investors who believe the travel secular recovery thesis still has legs Those seeking exposure to the premiumisation of air travel and the SkyMiles monetisation value chain Avoid Risk-averse or capital-preservation investors who cannot tolerate cyclical earnings volatility Those who believe current oil/commodity prices will spike materially in the next 12 months Investors with short holding periods; airline stocks are notoriously mean-reverting and can be rangebound for years

recommendation.text:
Rating: HOLD
    Delta is one of the most attractively valued major US airlines, trading at under 10x trailing earnings with improving free cash flow and a world-class loyalty asset. The SkyMiles-American Express partnership renewal removes a major overhang and provides multi-year revenue visibility. The shares offer compelling upside to $85-90 on a 12-month horizon with a meaningful floor provided by the asset-intensive but cash-generative nature of the business at current prices.
    
      FrameworkDetail
      Entry point$67.82 (current); attractive entry at under 10x earnings with improving fundamentals
      Target price (12 months)$82-$90 based on 11-12x base case EPS of $7.50-$8.00
      Stop-loss$37.00 (below the 52-week low; would indicate a fundamental deterioration in the travel demand backdrop)
      Position sizingDeep-value cyclical position; 1.5-2% portfolio weight; do not overload given sector-specific risk
      Key trigger to upgradeQ1 2026 earnings beat above $1.90 per share; transatlantic summer yields ahead of expectations; confirmation of above-consensus free cash flow generation

entryExit.text:
Delta Air Lines Inc (DAL) (DAL) — Entry / Exit Framework

HOLD zone: $62.39–$69.18 — current levels are fairly valued. No compelling reason to add or trim here.
Add / Initiate below $62.39 — material pullback that improves risk/reward.
Trim / Take Profits above $69.18 — approaching the 52-week high at $76.39. Risk/reward no longer favourable for new capital.
Stop-loss consideration: A sustained close below $38.02 (52-week low) would warrant reassessment.
